title: InterGawdStateBundle
description: Three state files used by the inter-Gawd protocol. Each is a separate JSON document on disk; this schema describes their union for reference. Per spec §13 and handoff E4.

household_gawds.description: File: ~/.gawd/state/household-gawds.json. Peers known to this Gawd. Populated during onboarding or via a future /household slash command (§17.9 — discovery mechanism is open). The gossip channel feature degrades gracefully when this is empty per §17.9.

peers.description: Array of peer Gawd descriptors. May be empty (single-Gawd household).

gawd_id.description: Peer Gawd's stable identifier (matches the 'from' field that peer emits in GAWD-MSG headers).

telegram_bot_id.description: Peer's Telegram bot user ID (numeric string form). Used for opt-in bot-to-bot DMs per Bot API 10.0.

telegram_bot_username.description: Peer's @-username without the leading @ (optional but useful for invites).

added_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this peer was added.

gossip_channel.description: File: ~/.gawd/state/gossip-channel.json. Set when the first /quiet invocation in this household has caused channel creation. Absent until then.

channel_id.description: Telegram chat_id of the gossip channel (negative for groups/supergroups). Stored as string to avoid 64-bit-int handling issues across jq/python.

channel_title.description: Display name of the gossip channel (e.g., 'greenlee-gossip').

invite_link.description: Telegram invite link for the channel, if created. Used to onboard peer bots and the Prophit. Optional.

created_by_gawd_id.description: Which Gawd in the household created the channel (first /quiet recipient).

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the channel record was first written.

peer_invitations.description: Audit trail of bot-to-bot opt-in attempts per peer.

inter_gawd_routing.description: File: ~/.gawd/state/inter-gawd-routing.json. Tracks current quiet/loud routing for this Gawd. Absent → default (loud, main channel).

mode.description: 'loud' = inter-Gawd traffic in main channel (default); 'quiet' = in gossip channel.

set_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the current mode was set.

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when /quiet automatically lifts. Absent → indefinite (or N/A for 'loud').

set_by_prophit_id.description: Telegram user ID of the Prophit who issued the slash command.
